TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi beforefieldinit VirtualMachine extends Azure.Provisioning.Primitives.ProvisionableResource

Docs.summary: VirtualMachine.

MemberSignature.Value: public VirtualMachine (string bicepIdentifier, string? resourceVersion = default);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(string bicepIdentifier, string resourceVersion) cil managed
Public Sub New (bicepIdentifier As String, Optional resourceVersion As String = Nothing)

Docs.param: The the Bicep identifier name of the VirtualMachine resource. This can be used to refer to the resource in expressions, but is not the Azure name of the resource. This value can contain letters, numbers, and underscores.
Docs.summary: Creates a new VirtualMachine.
Specifies additional capabilities enabled or disabled on the virtual machine.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property AvailabilitySetId As BicepValue(Of ResourceIdentifier)

Docs.summary: Gets or sets Id.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property BillingMaxPrice As BicepValue(Of Double)
member this.BillingMaxPrice : Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue<double> with get, set

Docs.summary:
Specifies the maximum price you are willing to pay for a Azure Spot VM/VMSS. This price is in US Dollars.

This price will be compared with the current Azure Spot price for the VM size. Also, the prices are compared at the time of create/update of Azure Spot VM/VMSS and the operation will only succeed if the maxPrice is greater than the current Azure Spot price.

The maxPrice will also be used for evicting a Azure Spot VM/VMSS if the current Azure Spot price goes beyond the maxPrice after creation of VM/VMSS.

Possible values are:

- Any decimal value greater than zero. Example: 0.01538

-1 – indicates default price to be up-to on-demand.

You can set the maxPrice to -1 to indicate that the Azure Spot VM/VMSS should not be evicted for price reasons. Also, the default max price is -1 if it is not provided by you.

Minimum api-version: 2019-03-01.

Boot Diagnostics is a debugging feature which allows you to view Console Output and Screenshot to diagnose VM status. **NOTE**: If storageUri is being specified then ensure that the storage account is in the same region and subscription as the VM. You can easily view the output of your console log. Azure also enables you to see a screenshot of the VM from the hypervisor.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property CapacityReservationGroupId As BicepValue(Of ResourceIdentifier)

Docs.summary: Gets or sets Id.

MemberSignature.Value: .method familyhidebysig virtual instance void DefineProvisionableProperties() cil managed

Docs.summary: Define all the provisionable properties of VirtualMachine.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property ETag As BicepValue(Of String)

Docs.summary: Etag is property returned in Create/Update/Get response of the VM, so that customer can supply it in the header to ensure optimistic updates.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property EvictionPolicy As BicepValue(Of VirtualMachineEvictionPolicyType)

Docs.summary: Specifies the eviction policy for the Azure Spot virtual machine and Azure Spot scale set. For Azure Spot virtual machines, both 'Deallocate' and 'Delete' are supported and the minimum api-version is 2019-03-01. For Azure Spot scale sets, both 'Deallocate' and 'Delete' are supported and the minimum api-version is 2017-10-30-preview.
The extended location of the Virtual Machine.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property ExtensionsTimeBudget As BicepValue(Of String)
member this.ExtensionsTimeBudget : Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue<string> with get, set

Docs.summary: Specifies the time alloted for all extensions to start. The time duration should be between 15 minutes and 120 minutes (inclusive) and should be specified in ISO 8601 format. The default value is 90 minutes (PT1H30M). Minimum api-version: 2020-06-01.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Azure.Provisioning.Compute.VirtualMachine FromExisting (string bicepIdentifier, string? resourceVersion = default);
.method public static hidebysig class Azure.Provisioning.Compute.VirtualMachine FromExisting(string bicepIdentifier, string resourceVersion) cil managed
Public Shared Function FromExisting (bicepIdentifier As String, Optional resourceVersion As String = Nothing) As VirtualMachine
static member FromExisting : string * string -> Azure.Provisioning.Compute.VirtualMachine

Docs.param: The the Bicep identifier name of the VirtualMachine resource. This can be used to refer to the resource in expressions, but is not the Azure name of the resource. This value can contain letters, numbers, and underscores.
Docs.summary: Creates a reference to an existing VirtualMachine.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property GalleryApplications As BicepList(Of VirtualMachineGalleryApplication)

Docs.summary: Specifies the gallery applications that should be made available to the VM/VMSS.
Specifies the hardware settings for the virtual machine.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property HostGroupId As BicepValue(Of ResourceIdentifier)

Docs.summary: Gets or sets Id.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property HostId As BicepValue(Of ResourceIdentifier)

Docs.summary: Gets or sets Id.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property Id As BicepValue(Of ResourceIdentifier)

Docs.summary: Gets the Id.
The identity of the virtual machine, if configured.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property InstanceView As VirtualMachineInstanceView

Docs.summary: The virtual machine instance view.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property LicenseType As BicepValue(Of String)
member this.LicenseType : Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue<string> with get, set

Docs.summary:
Specifies that the image or disk that is being used was licensed on-premises.

Possible values for Windows Server operating system are:

Windows_Client

Windows_Server

Possible values for Linux Server operating system are:

RHEL_BYOS (for RHEL)

SLES_BYOS (for SUSE)

For more information, see [Azure Hybrid Use Benefit for Windows Server](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/windows/hybrid-use-benefit-licensing)

[Azure Hybrid Use Benefit for Linux Server](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/linux/azure-hybrid-benefit-linux)

Minimum api-version: 2015-06-15.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property Location As BicepValue(Of AzureLocation)

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the Location.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property ManagedBy As BicepValue(Of String)

Docs.summary: ManagedBy is set to Virtual Machine Scale Set(VMSS) flex ARM resourceID, if the VM is part of the VMSS. This property is used by platform for internal resource group delete optimization.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property Name As BicepValue(Of String)
member this.Name : Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue<string> with get, set

Docs.summary: The name of the virtual machine.
Specifies the network interfaces of the virtual machine.
Specifies the operating system settings used while creating the virtual machine. Some of the settings cannot be changed once VM is provisioned.
Placement section specifies the user-defined constraints for virtual machine hardware placement. This property cannot be changed once VM is provisioned. Minimum api-version: 2024-11-01.

MemberSignature.Value: member this.Plan : Azure.Provisioning.Compute.ComputePlan with get, set

Docs.summary: Specifies information about the marketplace image used to create the virtual machine. This element is only used for marketplace images. Before you can use a marketplace image from an API, you must enable the image for programmatic use. In the Azure portal, find the marketplace image that you want to use and then click **Want to deploy programmatically, Get Started ->**. Enter any required information and then click **Save**.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property PlatformFaultDomain As BicepValue(Of Integer)
member this.PlatformFaultDomain : Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue<int> with get, set

Docs.summary: Specifies the scale set logical fault domain into which the Virtual Machine will be created. By default, the Virtual Machine will by automatically assigned to a fault domain that best maintains balance across available fault domains. This is applicable only if the 'virtualMachineScaleSet' property of this Virtual Machine is set. The Virtual Machine Scale Set that is referenced, must have 'platformFaultDomainCount' greater than 1. This property cannot be updated once the Virtual Machine is created. Fault domain assignment can be viewed in the Virtual Machine Instance View. Minimum api‐version: 2020‐12‐01.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property Priority As BicepValue(Of VirtualMachinePriorityType)

Docs.summary: Specifies the priority for the virtual machine. Minimum api-version: 2019-03-01.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property ProvisioningState As BicepValue(Of String)

Docs.summary: The provisioning state, which only appears in the response.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property ProximityPlacementGroupId As BicepValue(Of ResourceIdentifier)

Docs.summary: Gets or sets Id.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property Resources As BicepList(Of VirtualMachineExtension)

Docs.summary: The virtual machine child extension resources.
Specifies Redeploy, Reboot and ScheduledEventsAdditionalPublishingTargets Scheduled Event related configurations for the virtual machine.
Specifies Scheduled Event related configurations.
Specifies the Security related profile settings for the virtual machine.
Specifies the storage settings for the virtual machine disks.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property SystemData As SystemData

Docs.summary: Gets the SystemData.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property Tags As BicepDictionary(Of String)

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the Tags.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property TimeCreated As BicepValue(Of DateTimeOffset)

Docs.summary: Specifies the time at which the Virtual Machine resource was created. Minimum api-version: 2021-11-01.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property UserData As BicepValue(Of String)
member this.UserData : Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue<string> with get, set

Docs.summary: UserData for the VM, which must be base-64 encoded. Customer should not pass any secrets in here. Minimum api-version: 2021-03-01.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property VirtualMachineScaleSetId As BicepValue(Of ResourceIdentifier)

Docs.summary: Gets or sets Id.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property VmId As BicepValue(Of String)

Docs.summary: Specifies the VM unique ID which is a 128-bits identifier that is encoded and stored in all Azure IaaS VMs SMBIOS and can be read using platform BIOS commands.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Property Zones As BicepList(Of String)
member this.Zones : Azure.Provisioning.BicepList<string> with get, set

Docs.summary: The availability zones.
